context.description: Chapter 2 introduces pattern recognition as a central feature of consciousness and links it to neuroplasticity and habit formation. The author describes a long-term experiment that began with Christian symbolism and the number 666, which gradually trained attention toward recurring signals. The passage presents an autobiographical reflection on how repeated focus can reshape perception.
normalization_notes: Translated German headings and prose into English control-language values.
Promoted chapter and section titles from visible headings.

fields.section_title: The wonderful world of the invisible supercomputer: pattern recognition

rows.paragraph_text: Chapter 2 opens with a discussion of pattern recognition as a basic strength of the mind. The author recounts a long experiment shaped by Christian symbolism and the number 666, then explains how repeated attention can reinforce neural pathways and habits.

The passage argues that focusing on a signal over time can make the brain assign it persistent importance even after the stimulus is gone. The visible example is a personal account of how 666 seemed to appear more often after deliberate attention to it.
